title: pinus flexilis subsp. reflexa
intro: Pinus flexilis subsp. reflexa, also known as limber pine, is a plant species that belongs to the Pinaceae family and is native to the western parts of North America. The limber pine has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, reducing fever, and fighting off infections. The health benefits of limber pine include its ability to improve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Limber pine can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health benefits.
